title: Scope E T-E1 (Option 2 — root cause): fix reconcileKnowledge dual-root scan + force-write on revision drift
description: Root-cause fix in reconcileKnowledge: (1) extend findRuleFiles to scan personal layer (~/.fabric/knowledge/) alongside team root, mirroring findKnowledgeFiles dual-root pattern in knowledge-meta-builder.ts:335-383; (2) after the per-file loop, compare on-disk meta.revision_hash to derived revision via buildKnowledgeMeta — if mismatch, force writeKnowledgeMeta even when events.length === 0. Fixes the per-file-content-hash gate that previously suppressed top-level schema/revision drift fixes.

files.change: Extend findRuleFiles to scan BOTH team root (existing) AND personal root (`resolvePersonalRoot()/.fabric/knowledge/`). Mirror findKnowledgeFiles in knowledge-meta-builder.ts:335-383: iterate over [(teamRoot, TEAM_PREFIX), (personalRoot, PERSONAL_PREFIX)], list .md files in each subdir, build relative paths using the appropriate prefix convention. Verify downstream consumer (the for-loop at line 475) handles personal: prefix correctly — it currently joins with projectRoot, so personal-layer paths need either prefix-aware handling OR pass through a resolveContentRefPath-style helper. Pick whichever approach matches what readMetaEntries returns at line 137 (it already includes content_ref paths with PERSONAL_CONTENT_REF_PREFIX, so the comparison should naturally work).

files.target: reconcileKnowledge at lines 460-512, especially the events.length gate at line 508
files.change: After the per-file processing loop completes, compute derived revision: `const derived = await buildKnowledgeMeta(projectRoot); const onDiskMeta = await readAgentsMeta(projectRoot); const revisionDrift = onDiskMeta && derived.meta.revision !== onDiskMeta.revision`. Replace the gate `if (events.length > 0)` with `if (events.length > 0 || revisionDrift)`. When triggered by revisionDrift alone (events empty), still emit a `meta_reconciled` summary event with a synthetic field like `force_write_reason: 'revision_drift'` (or extend the existing event shape minimally — coordinate with T-D1 event schema patterns). Append the resulting reconciled_files (may be empty when only revision drift).

Tests: (1) stale meta with no per-file content drift → reconcile DOES write the new meta (regression for the events.length gate bug); (2) personal-layer KP-DEC-9999 entry on disk → appears in agents.meta.json after reconcile (regression for findRuleFiles team-only bug); (3) idempotent re-run: after first reconcile, second reconcile is no-op (no extra writes, no extra events); (4) team + personal both present → meta indexes both layers correctly; (5) original drift path (per-file content drift) still works (no regression).

implementation: Read knowledge-meta-builder.ts findKnowledgeFiles (lines 335-383) to extract the dual-root scan pattern
Refactor findRuleFiles to mirror that pattern, returning paths with appropriate prefix conventions
Verify downstream consumers (reconcileKnowledge loop, processSingleFile) handle prefixed paths — likely already work via readMetaEntries path matching since meta already stores prefixed content_refs
Add the post-loop revision-drift check in reconcileKnowledge
Extend writeKnowledgeMeta call condition to include revisionDrift case
Update event emission to include force_write_reason when triggered by revisionDrift only
Write 5 new tests covering both bugs (team-only scan + per-file gate) plus regression coverage
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/server test knowledge-sync` to confirm green
Verify no regression in doctor.test.ts (since doctor.ts:1048 calls reconcileKnowledge)

reference.examples: knowledge-meta-builder.ts:335-383 is the canonical dual-root scan; mirror its iteration over [(teamRoot, TEAM_PREFIX), (personalRoot, PERSONAL_PREFIX)]

criteria: findRuleFiles returns entries from BOTH .fabric/knowledge/ AND ~/.fabric/knowledge/ (verified via personal-layer regression test)
reconcileKnowledge writes agents.meta.json when on-disk revision !== derived revision, even when per-file events empty
Personal-layer files (e.g., KP-DEC-*) appear in agents.meta.json after reconcile
5 new tests pass; existing knowledge-sync.test.ts + doctor.test.ts green
MCP startup ensureKnowledgeFresh callers benefit transparently (no callsite changes needed)

rationale.chosen_approach: Option 2 (root cause) — fix findRuleFiles dual-root + reconcile revision-drift gate
alternatives_considered: Option 1 (surgical doctor.ts swap to writeKnowledgeMeta for stale codes) — leaves MCP startup + future callers broken
No fix relying solely on T-D1 auto-heal — write-side flows still broken
decision_factors: Pre-user clean-slate allows the bigger fix
rationale.tradeoffs: Larger blast radius (touched by doctor + MCP startup) — mitigated by 5 unit tests + T8 dogfood pass

risks.description: findRuleFiles return-type change breaks downstream callers expecting team-only paths
risks.mitigation: Pre-flight grep all findRuleFiles consumers; align prefix convention with existing knowledge-meta-builder.findKnowledgeFiles signature; run full server test suite before commit
risks.fallback: If unforeseen breakage emerges, split into two callsite-scoped helpers (findTeamRuleFiles + findPersonalRuleFiles) and call both explicitly in reconcileKnowledge

risks.description: force-write on revision drift could thrash if buildKnowledgeMeta and on-disk revision algorithms disagree spuriously
risks.mitigation: Verify buildKnowledgeMeta uses the same computeRevision path that wrote the meta originally; add an explicit assertion in test 3 that idempotency holds after one reconcile
risks.fallback: Add a max-write-per-reconcile guard (write at most once per call); log a warning if repeated revision drift detected in single process

manual_checks: Pre-seed a stub at ~/.fabric/knowledge/decisions/KP-DEC-9999.md, run fab doctor --fix, observe entry appears in agents.meta.json
Manually corrupt agents.meta.json revision string (not per-file content), run fab doctor --fix, observe meta gets rewritten
success_metrics: doctor.test.ts unaffected (reconcileKnowledge call from doctor.ts:1048 still works)
MCP startup ensureKnowledgeFresh tests (if scoped) green
